title: Prompt Injection in Automated R\'esum\'e Screening with Large Language Models: Single and Multi-Injection Settings
abstract: arXiv:2606.27287v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to screen and rank job applicants, creating incentives for candidates to strategically manipulate algorithmic hiring systems. We study prompt injection in automated r\'esum\'e screening, defined as subtle self-promotional text that introduces no new qualifications but is designed to influence LLM evaluations. Using controlled experiments, we show that prompt injection reliably improves applicant rankings when r\'esum\'e quality is homogeneous and few candidates inject. However, its effectiveness rapidly diminishes as more candidates inject, collapsing when manipulation becomes widespread. When candidate quality is heterogeneous, prompt injection is less effective on average, but can occasionally allow lower-quality candidates to outrank higher-quality ones, raising fairness concerns. Overall, LLM-based screening is most vulnerable when manipulation is rare and candidate quality differenc

When candidate quality is heterogeneous, prompt injection is less effective on average, but can occasionally allow lower-quality candidates to outrank higher-quality ones, raising fairness concerns. translated_title: 大型语言模型自动化简历筛选中的提示注入：单注入与多注入场景研究
translated_abstract: 大型语言模型（LLMs）正越来越多地被用于筛选和排序求职者，这促使候选人策略性地操纵算法招聘系统。我们研究了自动化简历筛选中的提示注入，即一种隐蔽的自我推销文本，它不引入新的资质，但旨在影响LLM的评估。通过控制实验，我们表明当简历质量同质且只有少数候选人注入时，提示注入能可靠地提升申请人排名。然而，随着更多候选人使用注入，其效果迅速减弱，当操纵变得普遍时，效果会完全崩溃。当候选人质量异质时，提示注入平均效果较低，但偶尔可能使低质量候选人排名超过高质量候选人，从而引发公平性问题。总体而言，当操纵行为稀少且候选人质量存在差异时，基于LLM的筛选最为脆弱。
tldr: 本研究揭示了大型语言模型在简历筛选中的提示注入攻击效果：在少数人使用时能提升排名，但广泛使用后失效，且在候选人质量参差不齐时可能破坏公平性。
en_tdlr: This study reveals that prompt injection in LLM-based resume screening effectively boosts rankings when used by few candidates, but collapses under widespread manipulation and can undermine fairness when candidate quality varies.
